data.title: Eurozone GDP expands 0.4% on quarter in Q3
data.description:
The Eurozone GDP expanded 0.4% on quarter in Q3, the strongest growth rate in two years, following a 0.2% rise in Q2 and in line with the first estimate.

ETFs: (EWG), (NYSE:GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: DOCU, ULTA, LULU, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower in premarket trading on Friday as investors eagerly awaited the jobs report for hints on future Federal Reserve policy.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • DocuSign (DOCU) shares jumped nearly 15% after the company posted strong FQ3 results and an upbeat FQ4 outlook. In Q3, subscription revenue rose 8% Y/Y to $734.7M, while professional services and other revenue grew 11% Y/Y to $20.1M. Billings increased 9% Y/Y to $752.3M. For FQ4, the company guided revenue in the range of $758M-$762M, surpassing the consensus of $756.2M.
  • Ulta Beauty (ULTA) shares surged 10% as it beat sales and profit expectations, turning negative comparable sales in Q2 into a 0.6% gain in Q3, outperforming the expected 2.0% decline. For the year, ULTA raised its sales outlook to $11.1B-$11.2B (up from $11.0B-$11.2B) and profit guidance to $23.20-$23.75 per share (vs. prior $22.60-$23.50), both above consensus estimates of $11.17B and $23.13. Additionally, it improved its operating margin forecast to 12.9%-13.1% (from 12.7%-13.0%) and adjusted comparable sales expectations to -1% to flat, beating projections of a 0.78% decline.
  • GitLab (GTLB) shares rose as much as 8% after surpassing FQ3 expectations and announcing a new CEO. For Q4, the company forecasts earnings of $0.22-$0.23 per share and revenue of $205M-$206M, exceeding consensus of $0.14 EPS and $204.42M in revenue. GitLab also raised its full-year guidance, now projecting adjusted EPS of $0.63-$0.64 (up from $0.45-$0.47), above the consensus of $0.47, and revenue of $753M-$754M (up from $742M-$744M), surpassing the consensus of $746.14M.
  • Lululemon (LULU) shares climbed 9% following robust Q3 results, driven by better-than-expected profit and sales, along with double-digit international comparable sales growth. The company raised its FY24 guidance, projecting revenue of $10.452B-$10.487B (up from $10.38B-$10.48B) and earnings of $14.08-$14.16 (up from $13.95-$14.15), both surpassing estimates of $10.43B and $14.04. For Q4, the company forecasts revenue of $3.475B-$3.510B and earnings of $5.56-$5.64 per share, compared to the consensus of $3.50B and $5.62. Additionally, the company expanded its share buyback program by $1B, further boosting investor confidence.

Biggest stock losers

  • UiPath (PATH) shares fell over 6% as its FQ4 guidance overshadowed a stronger-than-expected FQ3, which saw revenue and earnings surpass market expectations and annual recurring revenue (ARR) grow 17% Y/Y. For FQ4, UiPath forecasted revenue of $422M-$427M, aligning closely with the $424.03M consensus, and projected ARR in the range of $1.669B-$1.674B as of January 31, 2025.

data.title: Italy's iGenius unveils Nvidia DGX AI supercomputer with Grace Blackwell chips
data.description:
On Thursday, Italian AI startup iGenius unveiled Colosseum — one of the world's largest Nvidia DGX SuperPOD AI supercomputers with Nvidia Grace Blackwell Superchips.

"IGenius will use Colosseum to build advanced AI applications for customers worldwide to support highly regulated industry use cases in financial services, healthcare and public sector," said an Nvidia spokesperson in an email to Seeking Alpha.

The supercomputer features Nvidia DGX GB200 systems containing thousands of NVIDIA Grace Blackwell Superchips designed for real-time trillion-parameter inference and training, iGenius said in a press release on Thursday.

Colosseum will also be used to build advanced AI applications, including training open-source generative AI and trillion-plus-parameter large language models, or LLMs, according to iGenius.

Colosseum will be located in Europe and features state-of-the-art liquid cooling. The supercomputer is expected to deliver 115 exaflops of computational performance, all powered by renewable energy from Italy, iGenius added.

"We designed this supercomputer for unicorn use cases that can deliver $1B+ in value," said iGenius CEO Uljan Sharka.

Built with the Nvidia's AI Enterprise software platform, Nemotron models and the NeMo framework, the iGenius AI models will be offered as Nvidia NIM microservices.

IGenius said it plans to offer customers the option to deploy the models and microservices as fully managed applications, which can include cloud-based or on-premises Nvidia accelerated computing to meet industry and government regulations for AI.

data.title: Yum China announces $360 million share buyback plan
data.description:
• Yum China (NYSE:YUMC) has announced share repurchase agreements totaling $360 million for the first half of 2025, starting January 6, 2025.

  • The repurchase plan includes US$290 million in the U.S. and approximately HK$550 million in Hong Kong.

  • The agreements are part of a broader capital allocation plan to return US$4.5 billion to shareholders through 2024-2026.

  • In the first 11 months of 2024, the company has already returned a record US$1.37 billion to shareholders, including US$1.18 billion in share repurchases and US$187 million in cash dividends. The company has repurchased 30 million shares, representing approximately 7% of outstanding shares as of December 31, 2023.

More on Yum China

data.title: Boeing yet to restart 737 Max production after labor strike - report
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) has not resumed 737 Max production after the labor strike and plans to slowly restart later this month, head of the Federal Aviation Administration Mike Whitaker told Reuters in an interview.

Whitaker met with Boeing (BA) CEO Kelly Ortberg this week and toured the 737 plant in Renton, Washington. "… we're four weeks post-strike, and they're still not producing airplanes, because they're focused on their workforce, training, making sure they have the supply chain sorted out."

Whitaker is planning another meeting in January after production of the 737 - Boeing's (BA) top-selling aircraft - gets underway.

The FAA capped Boeing's (BA) 737 production at 38 jets per month after a 737 Max 9 door plug debacle in January. Whitaker expects it'll likely take several months before Boeing (BA) gets close to the 38-plane limit.

As for the Max 7, FAA certification is being held up by an engine de-icing issue. Whitaker expects a proposed fix later this month.

He said it could take five years for Boeing (BA) to reform its safety culture, but it appears to be on the right track. "What I saw this week was really what I expected to see."

data.title: Teva to divest Teva-Takeda business venture in Japan to JKI
data.description:
• Teva Pharmaceutical (NYSE:TEVA) announced that it will divest Teva-Takeda, its business venture in Japan, to JKI Co., established by the fund managed and operated by private equity firm J-Will Partners Co., Ltd.
  • As per the deal, all shares of Teva Takeda Pharma Limited and its wholly owned subsidiary Teva Takeda Yakuhin Ltd. will be transferred to JKI.
  • The divestment, which includes generics and legacy products, will allow Teva to focus on bringing its innovative medicines to the Japanese market.
  • The divestment aligns with Teva's Pivot to Growth strategy to focus its business.
  • The deal is expected to complete by April 1, 2025.

  • All the employees of the business venture in Japan will remain employed, subject to the terms of the agreement.

data.title: European markets mixed, French stocks climb despite government collapse
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.2%

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.1%

France (CAC:IND) +0.6%

The trade gap in France narrowed to €7.7 billion in October from a downward revised $8.4 billion in September.

The Halifax House Price Index in the UK increased 1.3% month-over-month in November, the biggest rise since June 2022.

German industrial production fell by 1% month-over-month in October, missing expectations of a 1.2% rebound.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) was little changed +0.1% at the end of a busy and data-heavy week on Friday, which also saw the French government being toppled, plunging the eurozone’s second-largest economy into a period of deep political uncertainty.

Investors would next scan the U.S. payrolls data that could sway expectations around the Federal Reserve's policy move later this month.

Coming up in the session: Euro Area GDP data awaited shortly, while Canada's unemployment data and U.S. NFP figures were also on the radar.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis point to 4.19%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point at 2.11%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point to 4.28%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Tractor Supply unveils long-term growth targets, announces 5-for-1 stock split
data.description:
Tractor Supply Company (NASDAQ:TSCO) has updated its long-term targets in its Life Out Here 2030 growth strategy, and announced a 5-for-1 stock split.

Under the strategy, the company is targeting EPS growth of 8%-11% and comparable store sales increase of 3%-5%. It targets net sales growth of 6%-8%, and operating margin of 10%-10.5%. To note, the long-term targets do not represent guidance for any currently identifiable period.

Tractor Supply (TSCO) updated its total addressable market to about $225B from $180B, and its long-term store count to 3,200, an increase of 200 locations. It expects to open 90 new stores and around 10 new Petsense stores next year.

The farm supply retail chain also announced a 5-for-1 stock split "to make its stock more accessible to team members and investors." This is its fifth stock split since it went public in 1994.

Shareholders of record as of December 16 will receive four additional shares for each share held, which will be distributed after market close on December 19. The shares will begin trading on a stock-split adjusted basis on December 20.
